properties.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 22 at 4:25PM MDT until July 22 at 4:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 425 PM MDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over D.I.A Terminal
and Concourses, or 18 miles northeast of Denver, moving northeast at
15 mph.

HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees.

This severe thunderstorm will be near...
Denver International Airport, D.I.A Terminal and Concourses,
Commerce City, Denver and Aurora around 430 PM MDT.
properties.areaDesc: Adams, CO; Denver, CO; Weld, CO

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 4:23PM MDT until July 22 at 4:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
The Flood Advisory will expire at 430 PM MDT this afternoon for a
portion of north central Colorado, including the following area,
Larimer County.

The heavy rain has ended. Small stream flooding is no longer expected
to pose a threat. However, additional rainfall is possible this
evening. Please continue to heed any remaining closures.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 22 at 4:21PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 421 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 11
miles south of Guffey, or 39 miles southwest of Colorado Springs,
moving north at 15 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and half inch hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of north central
Fremont and southwestern Teller Counties.
properties.areaDesc: Northwestern Fremont County  Above 8500Ft; Western/Central Fremont County Below 8500 Ft; Teller County/Rampart Range above 7500fT/Pike's Peak Between 7500 And 11000 Ft; Canon City Vicinity/Eastern Fremont County

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 4:09PM MDT until July 22 at 6:15PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...South Central Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 615 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 408 PM MDT, Thunderstorms are ongoing in the area, with
moderate to heavy rainfall at times. Minor flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 1.5
inches of rain have fallen.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Estes Park.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:06PM MDT until July 22 at 7:15PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Southeastern Garfield County in west central Colorado...

* Until 715 PM MDT.

* At 406 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1 and 1.5 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 3 inches in 1
hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
New Castle.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:02PM MDT until July 22 at 6:00PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 402 PM MDT, Heavy rain has currently ended in the area. However,
additional showers and thunderstorms are expected this afternoon.
Between 1.5 and 3 inches of rain have fallen. Flash flooding is
ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar and automated gauges.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, gulches, roads,
and poor drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Pine Junction, Pine Grove and Shaffers Crossing.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:00PM MDT until July 22 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Lee and PL Gulch burn scar in...
Central Rio Blanco County in northwestern Colorado...

* Until 700 PM MDT.

* At 400 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scar. Between 0.3 and 1
inch of rain has fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars. The debris flow can
consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Lee and PL
Gulch Burn Scars.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Central Rio Blanco County
Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 3:59PM MDT until July 22 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northwestern Mesa County in west central Colorado...

* Until 700 PM MDT.

* At 359 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain
have fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Grand Junction and Redlands.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 3:30PM MDT until July 22 at 5:15PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...Central Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 515 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 329 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. While thunderstorms
have currently left the area, additional storms are expected
later this afternoon. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 0.5 and 1.5 inches of
rain have fallen.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Rustic.

properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Southeastern Garfield County in west central Colorado...

* Until 630 PM MDT.

* At 323 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. The expected rainfall rate is 2
to 3 inches in 1 hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 2:08PM MDT until July 22 at 5:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
East Central Montrose County in west central Colorado...

* Until 500 PM MDT.

* At 208 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across Montrose, stretching east over the South Rim
burn scar. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 3 inches in 1 hour.
Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are possible in the
warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the South Rim. The debris flow can consist of rock,
mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Montrose and the South Rim burn scar.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 1:46PM MDT until July 22 at 4:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
North Central Montrose County in west central Colorado...

* Until 445 PM MDT.

* At 146 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.7 and 1.2 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 2 inches in 1
hour. Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are possible in
the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of North Central Montrose County

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Dry Creek, Spring Creek and Coal Creek.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 11:04AM MDT until July 22 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire is
possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County Colorado, including
Dexter and Cutler Creeks and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1057 AM MDT, The probability of heavy rainfall will
increase after 12 PM this afternoon, lasting through the
evening. If thunderstorms develop over the Gold Mountain burn
scar, debris flows are likely within Cutler and Dexter Creek
drainages, including Highway 550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
